coding.display: Accession ID
ICH M11 Protocol Section 11 TRIAL OVERSIGHT AND OTHER GENERAL CONSIDERATIONS

text.div: [No text is intended here (heading only).]

section.title: Trial Oversight and Other General Considerations

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.1 Regulatory and Ethical Considerations

text.div: [Provide a high-level statement on the prevailing ethical, legal, and regulatory guidelines that will be applied throughout the trial. For example, This trial will be conducted in accordance with the protocol and with the following: • Ethical principles that have their origin in the Declaration of Helsinki for medical research involving human subjects • Consensus ethical principles derived from international guidelines including the Declaration of Helsinki and the Council for International Organisations of Medical Sciences (CIOMS) International Ethical Guidelines • ICH Good Clinical Practice (GCP) Guidelines • Applicable laws and regulations]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.2 Trial Oversight

text.div: [Concisely summarize the trial oversight listing the investigator and sponsor responsibilities not covered in other sections of the protocol which are essential for the operations of the trial, specifying the ones related to quality assurance.]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.2.1 Investigator Responsibilities

text.div: [Describe the investigator duties, including the oversight of duties delegated to a third party that may impact the trial conduct at sites, if applicable and if not addressed elsewhere.]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.2.2 Sponsor Responsibilities

text.div: [Describe the sponsor duties, including those to be transferred to a third party that may impact the investigators sites, if applicable.]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.3 Informed Consent Process

text.div: [Specify the key elements of the informed consent process, including any special needs and how these are addressed (for example, assent, capacity, legally acceptable representative, adolescents who may reach age of majority during the trial, pregnant participants and pregnant partners of participants).]
[Enter Description of Assent Process.]
[If enrollment in the trial may occur during an emergency in which the participant or their legally acceptable representative is not able or available to give consent, describe the consent process.]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.3.1 Informed Consent for Rescreening

text.div: [If participants can be rescreened as described in Section 5.6, state whether the participant needs to complete a new consent. Screen failure and rescreening should be clearly defined in the protocol, with cross reference to those definitions.]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.3.2 Informed Consent for Use of Remaining Samples in Exploratory Research

text.div: [If participants will be asked to consent to optional exploratory research using the remainder of mandatory samples, describe the use of remaining samples for optional exploratory research. If any exploratory research is planned and additional written consent regarding the use of remaining samples for exploratory research will be obtained, describe the consent process.]

section.title: Informed Consent for Use of Remaining Samples in Exploratory Research

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.4 Committees

text.div: [Briefly describe the administrative structure of committees that will be reviewing data while the trial is ongoing, and the type of committee (e.g., Dose Escalation Committee, Data Monitoring Committee or Data Safety Monitoring Board). Note that specific details may be required depending on local law or regulation. If applicable, Committee Charters may be cross referenced. If no committees are involved, state “Not applicable.”]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.5 Insurance and Indemnity

text.div: [Concisely summarize the arrangements for participants insurance and indemnity if not addressed in a separate agreement, if required by the applicable regulatory requirements.]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.6 Risk-Based Quality Management

text.div: [Describe the identified critical to quality factors, associated risks and risk mitigation strategies in the trial or refer to a separate document where this is described.]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.7 Data Governance

text.div: [Describe the key systems and processes for critical trial integrity, traceability and security including a summary of the monitoring approaches enabling accurate collection, reporting, monitoring, transfer, retention, and access if not addressed in separate agreement(s).]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.8 Data Protection

text.div: [Describe the measures to protect the privacy and confidentiality of personal information of trial participants in accordance with applicable regulatory requirements on personal data protection and any measures that should be taken in case of a data security breach.]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.9 Source Records

text.div: [State the importance of source records and expectation for traceability. Delineate expectations for investigators (e.g., maintain and ensure availability of essential records) and trial monitors (e.g., ensure participant protections, ensure that the trial is conducted according to GCP). Identify what constitutes source records and its origin or provide a reference to the location of this information, if contained in a separate document. Describe the provision for direct access to source records enabling clinical trial-related monitoring, audits and regulatory inspections, if not included in separate agreement(s).]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.10 Protocol Deviations

text.div: [Describe plans for detecting, reviewing, and reporting any deviations from the protocol or include reference to a separate document.]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.11 Early Site Closure

text.div: [No text is intended here (heading only).]
[List the sponsor’s rights to close a site early. Likewise, list the investigator’s rights to initiate early site closure.]
[List the criteria for early closure of a site by the sponsor or investigator.]

[List the responsibilities of the sponsor and investigator following early site closure, such as informing the ethics committee(s), and prompt notification of the participant and their transition to appropriate therapy and/or follow-up.]

coding.display: ICH M11 Protocol Section 11.12 Data Dissemination

text.div: [Describe whether the clinical trial will be registered in public databases, including reporting of results, if applicable.]
